# TanStack Query

TanStack Query (formerly React Query) is a powerful asynchronous state management library for web applications. It simplifies fetching, caching, synchronizing, and updating server state across React, Vue, Solid, Svelte, and Angular frameworks. The library is protocol-agnostic, supporting REST, GraphQL, and any Promise-based data fetching method.

The core functionality revolves around three main concepts: Queries (for fetching data), Mutations (for creating/updating/deleting data), and Query Invalidation (for marking data as stale and triggering refetches). TanStack Query handles complex challenges like request deduplication, background refetching, pagination, infinite scroll, optimistic updates, and offline support out of the box with zero configuration.

## useQuery - Basic Data Fetching

The `useQuery` hook is the primary way to fetch and cache data. It requires a unique query key and a query function that returns a promise. The hook returns an object containing the query status, data, and error information.

```tsx
import {
  QueryClient,
  QueryClientProvider,
  useQuery,
} from '@tanstack/react-query'

const queryClient = new QueryClient()

function App() {
  return (
    <QueryClientProvider client={queryClient}>
      <Todos />
    </QueryClientProvider>
  )
}

function Todos() {
  const { isPending, isError, data, error, isFetching } = useQuery({
    queryKey: ['todos'],
    queryFn: async () => {
      const response = await fetch('https://api.example.com/todos')
      if (!response.ok) throw new Error('Network response was not ok')
      return response.json()
    },
    staleTime: 5 * 60 * 1000, // Data considered fresh for 5 minutes
    gcTime: 10 * 60 * 1000, // Cache garbage collected after 10 minutes
    refetchOnWindowFocus: true, // Refetch when window regains focus
    retry: 3, // Retry failed requests 3 times
  })

  if (isPending) return <span>Loading...</span>
  if (isError) return <span>Error: {error.message}</span>

  return (
    <div>
      {isFetching && <span>Updating...</span>}
      <ul>
        {data.map((todo) => (
          <li key={todo.id}>{todo.title}</li>
        ))}
      </ul>
    </div>
  )
}
```

## Query Keys - Cache Identification

Query keys uniquely identify cached data and can be simple strings or complex arrays with variables. Keys are hashed deterministically, so object property order doesn't matter. Include all variables that affect the query function in the key.

```tsx
import { useQuery } from '@tanstack/react-query'

// Simple key for a list
const todosQuery = useQuery({
  queryKey: ['todos'],
  queryFn: fetchAllTodos,
})

// Key with ID for individual item
const todoQuery = useQuery({
  queryKey: ['todo', todoId],
  queryFn: () => fetchTodoById(todoId),
})

// Key with filter parameters
const filteredTodos = useQuery({
  queryKey: ['todos', { status: 'completed', page: 1, sortBy: 'date' }],
  queryFn: () => fetchTodos({ status: 'completed', page: 1, sortBy: 'date' }),
})

// These are considered equal (object key order doesn't matter):
// ['todos', { page: 1, status: 'active' }]
// ['todos', { status: 'active', page: 1 }]

// Dependent query with dynamic key
function TodoDetails({ todoId }) {
  const query = useQuery({
    queryKey: ['todo', todoId, 'details'],
    queryFn: () => fetchTodoDetails(todoId),
    enabled: !!todoId, // Only fetch when todoId exists
  })
  return <div>{query.data?.description}</div>
}
```

## useMutation - Data Modification

The `useMutation` hook handles create, update, and delete operations. It provides callbacks for handling success, error, and settlement states, enabling optimistic updates and cache invalidation.

```tsx
import { useMutation, useQueryClient } from '@tanstack/react-query'

function TodoForm() {
  const queryClient = useQueryClient()

  const mutation = useMutation({
    mutationFn: async (newTodo) => {
      const response = await fetch('https://api.example.com/todos', {
        method: 'POST',
        headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
        body: JSON.stringify(newTodo),
      })
      if (!response.ok) throw new Error('Failed to create todo')
      return response.json()
    },
    onMutate: async (newTodo) => {
      // Cancel outgoing refetches
      await queryClient.cancelQueries({ queryKey: ['todos'] })
      // Snapshot previous value
      const previousTodos = queryClient.getQueryData(['todos'])
      // Optimistically update cache
      queryClient.setQueryData(['todos'], (old) => [...old, { ...newTodo, id: Date.now() }])
      return { previousTodos }
    },
    onError: (err, newTodo, context) => {
      // Rollback on error
      queryClient.setQueryData(['todos'], context.previousTodos)
    },
    onSuccess: (data) => {
      console.log('Todo created:', data)
    },
    onSettled: () => {
      // Refetch after mutation settles
      queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ['todos'] })
    },
    retry: 3,
  })

  const handleSubmit = (e) => {
    e.preventDefault()
    mutation.mutate({ title: 'New Todo', completed: false })
  }

  return (
    <form onSubmit={handleSubmit}>
      <button type="submit" disabled={mutation.isPending}>
        {mutation.isPending ? 'Adding...' : 'Add Todo'}
      </button>
      {mutation.isError && <p>Error: {mutation.error.message}</p>}
      {mutation.isSuccess && <p>Todo added successfully!</p>}
    </form>
  )
}
```

## Query Invalidation - Cache Management

Query invalidation marks queries as stale and triggers refetches. You can invalidate by exact key, key prefix, or using predicate functions for fine-grained control.

```tsx
import { useQueryClient } from '@tanstack/react-query'

function CacheManager() {
  const queryClient = useQueryClient()

  // Invalidate all queries
  const invalidateAll = () => {
    queryClient.invalidateQueries()
  }

  // Invalidate by key prefix - matches ['todos'], ['todos', 1], ['todos', { status: 'active' }]
  const invalidateTodos = () => {
    queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ['todos'] })
  }

  // Invalidate exact key only - matches only ['todos']
  const invalidateExact = () => {
    queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ['todos'], exact: true })
  }

  // Invalidate with predicate function
  const invalidateCompleted = () => {
    queryClient.invalidateQueries({
      predicate: (query) =>
        query.queryKey[0] === 'todos' &&
        query.queryKey[1]?.status === 'completed',
    })
  }

  // Invalidate without refetching (just mark stale)
  const markStale = () => {
    queryClient.invalidateQueries({
      queryKey: ['todos'],
      refetchType: 'none',
    })
  }

  // Reset queries to initial state
  const resetTodos = () => {
    queryClient.resetQueries({ queryKey: ['todos'] })
  }

  // Remove queries from cache entirely
  const removeTodos = () => {
    queryClient.removeQueries({ queryKey: ['todos'] })
  }

  return (
    <div>
      <button onClick={invalidateTodos}>Refresh Todos</button>
      <button onClick={resetTodos}>Reset Todos</button>
    </div>
  )
}
```

## useInfiniteQuery - Pagination and Infinite Scroll

The `useInfiniteQuery` hook manages paginated data with support for infinite scroll, bi-directional pagination, and page limits.

```tsx
import { useInfiniteQuery } from '@tanstack/react-query'
import { useCallback, useRef } from 'react'

function InfiniteProjects() {
  const {
    data,
    error,
    fetchNextPage,
    fetchPreviousPage,
    hasNextPage,
    hasPreviousPage,
    isFetching,
    isFetchingNextPage,
    isFetchingPreviousPage,
    status,
  } = useInfiniteQuery({
    queryKey: ['projects'],
    queryFn: async ({ pageParam }) => {
      const response = await fetch(`/api/projects?cursor=${pageParam}&limit=10`)
      return response.json() // { data: [...], nextCursor: 123, prevCursor: 100 }
    },
    initialPageParam: 0,
    getNextPageParam: (lastPage) => lastPage.nextCursor ?? undefined,
    getPreviousPageParam: (firstPage) => firstPage.prevCursor ?? undefined,
    maxPages: 5, // Limit stored pages for performance
  })

  // Intersection Observer for infinite scroll
  const observerRef = useRef()
  const lastElementRef = useCallback(
    (node) => {
      if (isFetchingNextPage) return
      if (observerRef.current) observerRef.current.disconnect()
      observerRef.current = new IntersectionObserver((entries) => {
        if (entries[0].isIntersecting && hasNextPage) {
          fetchNextPage()
        }
      })
      if (node) observerRef.current.observe(node)
    },
    [isFetchingNextPage, hasNextPage, fetchNextPage]
  )

  if (status === 'pending') return <p>Loading...</p>
  if (status === 'error') return <p>Error: {error.message}</p>

  return (
    <div>
      {hasPreviousPage && (
        <button onClick={() => fetchPreviousPage()} disabled={isFetchingPreviousPage}>
          {isFetchingPreviousPage ? 'Loading...' : 'Load Previous'}
        </button>
      )}

      {data.pages.map((page, pageIndex) => (
        <div key={pageIndex}>
          {page.data.map((project, index) => {
            const isLast = pageIndex === data.pages.length - 1 && index === page.data.length - 1
            return (
              <div key={project.id} ref={isLast ? lastElementRef : null}>
                {project.name}
              </div>
            )
          })}
        </div>
      ))}

      {isFetchingNextPage && <p>Loading more...</p>}
      {!hasNextPage && <p>No more projects</p>}
    </div>
  )
}
```

## useSuspenseQuery - React Suspense Integration

The `useSuspenseQuery` hook integrates with React Suspense for declarative loading states. Data is guaranteed to be defined, with loading and error states handled by Suspense boundaries.

```tsx
import { useSuspenseQuery } from '@tanstack/react-query'
import { Suspense } from 'react'
import { ErrorBoundary } from 'react-error-boundary'
import { QueryErrorResetBoundary } from '@tanstack/react-query'

function TodoList() {
  // data is guaranteed to be defined here
  const { data } = useSuspenseQuery({
    queryKey: ['todos'],
    queryFn: fetchTodos,
  })

  return (
    <ul>
      {data.map((todo) => (
        <li key={todo.id}>{todo.title}</li>
      ))}
    </ul>
  )
}

function App() {
  return (
    <QueryErrorResetBoundary>
      {({ reset }) => (
        <ErrorBoundary
          onReset={reset}
          fallbackRender={({ error, resetErrorBoundary }) => (
            <div>
              <p>Something went wrong: {error.message}</p>
              <button onClick={resetErrorBoundary}>Try again</button>
            </div>
          )}
        >
          <Suspense fallback={<div>Loading todos...</div>}>
            <TodoList />
          </Suspense>
        </ErrorBoundary>
      )}
    </QueryErrorResetBoundary>
  )
}
```

## Prefetching - Performance Optimization

Prefetching loads data before it's needed, improving perceived performance. Use it in event handlers, components, or router integrations.

```tsx
import { useQuery, useQueryClient } from '@tanstack/react-query'

function ArticleList() {
  const queryClient = useQueryClient()

  // Prefetch on hover
  const prefetchArticle = (id) => {
    queryClient.prefetchQuery({
      queryKey: ['article', id],
      queryFn: () => fetchArticle(id),
      staleTime: 5 * 60 * 1000, // Only prefetch if data older than 5 min
    })
  }

  // Prefetch infinite query
  const prefetchComments = (articleId) => {
    queryClient.prefetchInfiniteQuery({
      queryKey: ['comments', articleId],
      queryFn: ({ pageParam }) => fetchComments(articleId, pageParam),
      initialPageParam: 0,
      pages: 2, // Prefetch first 2 pages
    })
  }

  const { data: articles } = useQuery({
    queryKey: ['articles'],
    queryFn: fetchArticles,
  })

  return (
    <ul>
      {articles?.map((article) => (
        <li
          key={article.id}
          onMouseEnter={() => prefetchArticle(article.id)}
          onFocus={() => prefetchArticle(article.id)}
        >
          <a href={`/article/${article.id}`}>{article.title}</a>
        </li>
      ))}
    </ul>
  )
}

// Manually prime cache with existing data
function primeCache(queryClient, articles) {
  articles.forEach((article) => {
    queryClient.setQueryData(['article', article.id], article)
  })
}
```

## QueryClient - Direct Cache Manipulation

The `QueryClient` provides methods for direct cache interaction, including fetching, setting, and managing query data programmatically.

```tsx
import { QueryClient } from '@tanstack/react-query'

const queryClient = new QueryClient({
  defaultOptions: {
    queries: {
      staleTime: 5 * 60 * 1000,
      gcTime: 10 * 60 * 1000,
      retry: 3,
      refetchOnWindowFocus: true,
    },
    mutations: {
      retry: 2,
    },
  },
})

// Fetch query (returns data or throws)
async function loadData() {
  try {
    const todos = await queryClient.fetchQuery({
      queryKey: ['todos'],
      queryFn: fetchTodos,
      staleTime: 10000,
    })
    console.log('Fetched:', todos)
  } catch (error) {
    console.error('Fetch failed:', error)
  }
}

// Get cached data synchronously
const cachedTodos = queryClient.getQueryData(['todos'])

// Set cached data directly
queryClient.setQueryData(['todos'], (oldData) => [...oldData, newTodo])

// Ensure data exists (fetch if missing)
const todos = await queryClient.ensureQueryData({
  queryKey: ['todos'],
  queryFn: fetchTodos,
})

// Get query state
const state = queryClient.getQueryState(['todos'])
console.log('Last updated:', state?.dataUpdatedAt)

// Set defaults for specific queries
queryClient.setQueryDefaults(['todos'], {
  queryFn: fetchTodos,
  staleTime: Infinity,
})

// Check fetching status
const fetchingCount = queryClient.isFetching()
const mutatingCount = queryClient.isMutating()

// Cancel all outgoing queries
await queryClient.cancelQueries({ queryKey: ['todos'] })

// Clear entire cache
queryClient.clear()
```

## Vue Query Integration

Vue Query provides the same powerful data fetching capabilities with Vue.js composition API integration.

```vue
<script setup>
import { useQueryClient, useQuery, useMutation } from '@tanstack/vue-query'

const queryClient = useQueryClient()

// Query
const { isPending, isError, data, error } = useQuery({
  queryKey: ['todos'],
  queryFn: async () => {
    const response = await fetch('https://api.example.com/todos')
    return response.json()
  },
})

// Mutation
const mutation = useMutation({
  mutationFn: async (newTodo) => {
    const response = await fetch('https://api.example.com/todos', {
      method: 'POST',
      headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
      body: JSON.stringify(newTodo),
    })
    return response.json()
  },
  onSuccess: () => {
    queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ['todos'] })
  },
})

function addTodo() {
  mutation.mutate({ id: Date.now(), title: 'New Todo' })
}
</script>

<template>
  <span v-if="isPending">Loading...</span>
  <span v-else-if="isError">Error: {{ error.message }}</span>
  <ul v-else>
    <li v-for="todo in data" :key="todo.id">{{ todo.title }}</li>
  </ul>
  <button @click="addTodo" :disabled="mutation.isPending">
    {{ mutation.isPending ? 'Adding...' : 'Add Todo' }}
  </button>
</template>
```

## Solid Query Integration

Solid Query offers TanStack Query for SolidJS with reactive primitives. Arguments must be wrapped in functions to preserve reactivity.

```tsx
import { QueryClient, QueryClientProvider, useQuery, createMutation } from '@tanstack/solid-query'
import { Switch, Match, For, createSignal } from 'solid-js'

const queryClient = new QueryClient()

function TodoApp() {
  const [filter, setFilter] = createSignal('all')

  // Note: options must be wrapped in a function for reactivity
  const query = useQuery(() => ({
    queryKey: ['todos', filter()],
    queryFn: () => fetchTodos(filter()),
  }))

  const mutation = createMutation(() => ({
    mutationFn: (newTodo) => createTodo(newTodo),
    onSuccess: () => {
      queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ['todos'] })
    },
  }))

  return (
    <QueryClientProvider client={queryClient}>
      <div>
        <select onChange={(e) => setFilter(e.target.value)}>
          <option value="all">All</option>
          <option value="active">Active</option>
          <option value="completed">Completed</option>
        </select>

        <Switch>
          <Match when={query.isPending}>
            <p>Loading...</p>
          </Match>
          <Match when={query.isError}>
            <p>Error: {query.error.message}</p>
          </Match>
          <Match when={query.isSuccess}>
            <For each={query.data}>
              {(todo) => <div>{todo.title}</div>}
            </For>
          </Match>
        </Switch>

        <button onClick={() => mutation.mutate({ title: 'New Todo' })}>
          Add Todo
        </button>
      </div>
    </QueryClientProvider>
  )
}
```

## Svelte Query Integration

Svelte Query provides TanStack Query for Svelte with reactive stores. Like Solid Query, options must be wrapped in functions.

```svelte
<script lang="ts">
  import { QueryClient, QueryClientProvider, createQuery, createMutation, useQueryClient } from '@tanstack/svelte-query'

  const queryClient = new QueryClient()
</script>

<QueryClientProvider client={queryClient}>
  <TodoList />
</QueryClientProvider>

<!-- TodoList.svelte -->
<script lang="ts">
  import { createQuery, createMutation, useQueryClient } from '@tanstack/svelte-query'

  const client = useQueryClient()

  const query = createQuery(() => ({
    queryKey: ['todos'],
    queryFn: async () => {
      const response = await fetch('/api/todos')
      return response.json()
    },
  }))

  const mutation = createMutation(() => ({
    mutationFn: async (newTodo: { title: string }) => {
      const response = await fetch('/api/todos', {
        method: 'POST',
        body: JSON.stringify(newTodo),
      })
      return response.json()
    },
    onSuccess: () => {
      client.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ['todos'] })
    },
  }))

  function addTodo() {
    mutation.mutate({ title: 'New Todo' })
  }
</script>

<div>
  {#if query.isLoading}
    <p>Loading...</p>
  {:else if query.isError}
    <p>Error: {query.error.message}</p>
  {:else if query.isSuccess}
    {#each query.data as todo}
      <p>{todo.title}</p>
    {/each}
  {/if}

  <button on:click={addTodo} disabled={mutation.isPending}>
    {mutation.isPending ? 'Adding...' : 'Add Todo'}
  </button>
</div>
```

## Angular Query Integration

Angular Query provides TanStack Query for Angular with signal-based reactivity and dependency injection.

```typescript
// app.config.ts
import { provideHttpClient } from '@angular/common/http'
import { provideTanStackQuery, QueryClient } from '@tanstack/angular-query-experimental'

export const appConfig = {
  providers: [
    provideHttpClient(),
    provideTanStackQuery(new QueryClient()),
  ],
}

// todos.component.ts
import { Component, inject } from '@angular/core'
import { HttpClient } from '@angular/common/http'
import { injectQuery, injectMutation, QueryClient } from '@tanstack/angular-query-experimental'
import { lastValueFrom } from 'rxjs'

interface Todo {
  id: number
  title: string
  completed: boolean
}

@Component({
  selector: 'app-todos',
  template: `
    <div>
      @if (todosQuery.isPending()) {
        <span>Loading...</span>
      } @else if (todosQuery.isError()) {
        <span>Error: {{ todosQuery.error()?.message }}</span>
      } @else {
        <ul>
          @for (todo of todosQuery.data(); track todo.id) {
            <li>{{ todo.title }}</li>
          }
        </ul>
      }

      <button (click)="addTodo()" [disabled]="addMutation.isPending()">
        {{ addMutation.isPending() ? 'Adding...' : 'Add Todo' }}
      </button>
    </div>
  `,
})
export class TodosComponent {
  private http = inject(HttpClient)
  private queryClient = inject(QueryClient)

  todosQuery = injectQuery(() => ({
    queryKey: ['todos'],
    queryFn: () => lastValueFrom(this.http.get<Todo[]>('/api/todos')),
  }))

  addMutation = injectMutation(() => ({
    mutationFn: (todo: Partial<Todo>) =>
      lastValueFrom(this.http.post<Todo>('/api/todos', todo)),
    onSuccess: () => {
      this.queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ['todos'] })
    },
  }))

  addTodo() {
    this.addMutation.mutate({ title: 'New Todo', completed: false })
  }
}
```

## Optimistic Updates - UI Responsiveness

Optimistic updates provide instant UI feedback while mutations are in progress, with automatic rollback on errors.

```tsx
import { useMutation, useQueryClient, useMutationState } from '@tanstack/react-query'

function TodoList() {
  const queryClient = useQueryClient()

  // Approach 1: Optimistic update via cache
  const updateTodoMutation = useMutation({
    mutationFn: async (updatedTodo) => {
      const response = await fetch(`/api/todos/${updatedTodo.id}`, {
        method: 'PUT',
        body: JSON.stringify(updatedTodo),
      })
      return response.json()
    },
    onMutate: async (newTodo) => {
      await queryClient.cancelQueries({ queryKey: ['todos'] })
      const previousTodos = queryClient.getQueryData(['todos'])

      queryClient.setQueryData(['todos'], (old) =>
        old.map((todo) => (todo.id === newTodo.id ? newTodo : todo))
      )

      return { previousTodos }
    },
    onError: (err, newTodo, context) => {
      queryClient.setQueryData(['todos'], context.previousTodos)
    },
    onSettled: () => {
      queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ['todos'] })
    },
  })

  // Approach 2: Optimistic update via UI (simpler)
  const addTodoMutation = useMutation({
    mutationFn: (newTodo) => fetch('/api/todos', {
      method: 'POST',
      body: JSON.stringify(newTodo),
    }).then(r => r.json()),
    mutationKey: ['addTodo'],
    onSettled: () => queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ['todos'] }),
  })

  // Access mutation variables from another component
  const pendingTodos = useMutationState({
    filters: { mutationKey: ['addTodo'], status: 'pending' },
    select: (mutation) => mutation.state.variables,
  })

  const { data: todos } = useQuery({ queryKey: ['todos'], queryFn: fetchTodos })

  return (
    <ul>
      {todos?.map((todo) => (
        <li key={todo.id} style={{ opacity: todo.isPending ? 0.5 : 1 }}>
          {todo.title}
        </li>
      ))}
      {/* Show optimistic items */}
      {addTodoMutation.isPending && (
        <li style={{ opacity: 0.5 }}>{addTodoMutation.variables?.title}</li>
      )}
      {/* Or from mutation state */}
      {pendingTodos.map((todo) => (
        <li key={todo.id} style={{ opacity: 0.5 }}>{todo.title}</li>
      ))}
    </ul>
  )
}
```

## Summary

TanStack Query excels at managing server state in modern web applications. Its primary use cases include fetching and caching API data, handling complex pagination and infinite scroll scenarios, implementing optimistic updates for responsive UIs, prefetching data for improved performance, and synchronizing server state across components. The library supports multiple frameworks including React, Vue, Solid, Svelte, and Angular, with consistent APIs adapted to each framework's paradigms.

Integration patterns typically involve setting up a QueryClient provider at the application root, using query hooks in components for data fetching, implementing mutations for data modifications with cache invalidation, and leveraging advanced features like suspense, prefetching, and optimistic updates as needed. The framework-agnostic core ensures that knowledge transfers across different frontend technologies, while the extensive devtools and TypeScript support make debugging and development efficient.
